headline: US-Iran deal crushes oil risk premium, fuels risk-on rally; space sector rout deepens — TSLA, IP, WY all takes

market_state.line: SPY +0.54% / QQQ +0.65%, both above 50d; VXX stress 0.95 — low fear. Iran deal-driven crude drop supports dip entries but watch oil vol.

actions.line: Half size long — signal + research agree, clean risk; score 6, no watchlist keeps conviction low.
Half size long — materials momentum intact, mid-range RSI; research + risk aligned, signal neutral.
Quarter size long — signal agrees, research against; +1.47% 5d limits conviction, tight leash.

watch.line: -32.8% on 2.15x vol, MACD bearish — space sector lead to downside; no bounce setup yet.
-15.5% on 2.63x vol — heaviest relative volume in the space rout; watch for capitulation/flush.
-12.0% on 2.31x vol — unusual volume confirms distribution, not just sympathy sell.
+11.4% lone green outlier in a sea of red — no catalyst flagged, monitor for follow-through or fade.
-19.3% — airline/leasing names exposed to oil vol; Iran deal crude drop a double-edged read here.

changed: US-Iran diplomatic deal now dominant macro driver — crude risk premium unwinding, broad equity tailwind added since last briefing
Space sector rout intensifying: SPCE -32.8%, ASTS -15.5%, NAVN -12% all printing unusual volume overnight
caveats: AH/Pre-market scan as of 08:26 ET Jun 15 2026 — prices stale vs RTH open; gap risk elevated given Iran deal headline still developing.
Crude oil narrative is contradictory: one analyst camp calls risk premium over, another says shock 'far from over' — oil-linked entries face binary headline risk at open.
